creator: cem@freebsd.org
text:
On CURRENT:

$ LC_CTYPE=ja_JP.UTF-8 LC_COLLATE=C LANG=C locale
LANG=C
LC_CTYPE=ja_JP.UTF-8
LC_COLLATE="C"
LC_TIME="C"
LC_NUMERIC="C"
LC_MONETARY="C"
LC_MESSAGES="C"
LC_ALL=

sort(1) attempts to identify situations where it can run in fast, byte-compare only mode by looking only at LC_COLLATE.  The --debug option shows more information:

$ (echo иЂі ; echo и„і ; echo иЂі) | LC_CTYPE=ja_JP.UTF-8 LC_COLLATE=C LANG=C sort --debug
Memory to be used for sorting: 17100230656
Using collate rules of C locale
Byte sort is used
sort_method=radixsort
; offset=1
; k1=<иЂі>(1), k2=<и„і>(1); offset=1; s1=<иЂі>, s2=<и„і>; cmp1=0
; offset=1
; k1=<и„і>(1), k2=<иЂі>(1); offset=1; s1=<и„і>, s2=<иЂі>; cmp1=0
иЂі
и„і
иЂі

Both compares seem wrong.  The UTF-8 sequences share only the first byte, 0xe8.

In LC_CTYPE=C mode:

; offset=1
; k1=<иЂі>(3), k2=<и„і>(3); offset=1; s1=<иЂі>, s2=<и„і>; cmp1=-4
; offset=1
; k1=<и„і>(3), k2=<иЂі>(3); offset=1; s1=<и„і>, s2=<иЂі>; cmp1=4
; offset=1
; k1=<иЂі>(3), k2=<иЂі>(3); offset=1; s1=<иЂі>, s2=<иЂі>; cmp1=0
иЂі
иЂі
и„і

The comparisons look correct.  I will look a little more.  I think this is a bug, not design, but I am not sure yet.
